name: Fremont
metaTitle: Fremont Accident Guide — Local Hospitals, Courts & Rights
metaDescription: Injured in Fremont? Find local emergency hospitals, courts, and understand your rights under California personal injury law.
description: Fremont is a major South Bay city in Alameda County, anchored by manufacturing and technology industries that draw significant daily commuter and freight traffic. The I-880 corridor runs through the heart of the city and carries heavy commercial truck loads heading toward the Port of Oakland, creating elevated rear-end and sideswipe collision risk. I-680 bisects Fremont from north to south, connecting it to the broader Tri-Valley area and generating high-speed merge conflicts. State Route 84 links the city westward across the Dumbarton Bridge, adding cross-bay commute volume. Mission Boulevard and Fremont Boulevard serve as primary urban arterials, both prone to intersection conflicts and pedestrian exposure near shopping centers and BART stations. Mowry Avenue is a high-traffic commercial corridor connecting BART to the city interior. BART station proximity creates pedestrian and transit-related injury exposure at key station access points. Cyclists using the Washington Hospital area and Quarry Lakes trails also face road-sharing conflicts with commuter traffic.

courts.name: Alameda County Superior Court — Fremont Hall of Justice

commonAccidentTypes: Freeway rear-end and lane-change collisions on I-880 near freight routes
High-speed merge accidents on I-680 and SR-84 on-ramps
Pedestrian accidents near BART station entrances and Mowry Ave crossings
Intersection T-bone collisions on Mission Blvd and Fremont Blvd
Bicycle accidents along urban corridors near Washington Hospital
Commercial truck accidents on I-880 heading toward Port of Oakland
notableCorridors: I-880 — primary freight corridor with heavy truck volume toward Port of Oakland
I-680 — high-speed north-south commuter freeway with complex merge zones
SR-84 (Dumbarton Expressway) — cross-bay commute route with peak-hour congestion
Mission Blvd — urban arterial with high intersection conflict and pedestrian exposure
Fremont Blvd — commercial corridor with frequent turning conflict near shopping centers
Mowry Ave — BART-adjacent arterial with elevated pedestrian and cyclist activity
localNotes: Fremont is in Alameda County. Accidents involving city or county property, vehicles, or sidewalks require a government claim filed within 6 months of the injury date. Fremont Police Department traffic collision reports can be requested through the FPD Records Division. BART is a potential defendant in transit-related injury cases at Fremont, Warm Springs, and Centerville stations.
